comment_content: My favorite part of Neil's writing was that I couldn't see the conclusion. He would set these impossible situations for the lead characters to deal with. The production is really terrible, but the actors and story are  magnificent.  My favorite work by Neil to date.

comment_content:
For anyone reading this comment who has read the Sandman comics, did you enjoy them?
I loved season 1 of Sandman, and thinking about buying the first one or two comic stories. There looks to be a massive amount of content in the original comics - which sounds perfect for bingeing if I end up enjoying the format.
The Neil Gaiman controversy is a little concerning, but trying to separate art from the artist, especially in the case of the show with the cast and crew creating an amazing production from their hard work and dedication in the first season.
reply_content: I have misgivings about Gaiman now as well. I read the Sandman series about fifteen years ago and the series was great. It's full of spirituality (in a really strange way) and philosophy. It's extremely well written and is probably the best graphic novel series I have ever read. It's the kind of thing that will have you pondering reality after reading it. The only issue is that fifteen years after reading it the truth about Gaiman came out and I'm having trouble separating art from artiest especially when all of it brings Gaiman a ton of money. So in closing great series, not so great author.

But yeah, agree with you completely on not so great author.

@ There are 9 accusers total, but the sheer evidence, not mere number of accusers is what should conclude either guilt or innocence. 2 of the women signed an NDA before the acts were performed. new York Magazine (January 13, 2025) states: 

"Pavlovich signed an NDA with Gaiman and received $9,200 in payments, which she claims was part of an effort to silence her allegations of se*ual assault and abuse."

caroline Wallner:
Rolling Stone (August 2, 2024) reports:
"Wallner signed a non-disclosure agreement in return for a $275,000 payment, which included covering trauma-related costs, and was asked to leave the property."

4 of the other accusers are anonymous with no legal action reported when the incidents occured. That includes: 

K (Anonymous) who claimed she met gaiman at 18 and began a consensual relationship at 20, Claire ( Who had a consensual 10 month relationship) ,Rachel - who reports a consensual relationship but rough. 9th accusers story is unknown but is stated to be part of the group alledging conduct- The guardian

Now for the other known accusers: 
Katherine Kendall who claimed gaiman made unwanted advances on her, but payed her $60,000 for therapy, but no legal action was filed against him suprisingly. Now kendra stouts story has some has truth to it since she filed a police report because gaiman demonstrated "agressive and forceful intercourse when she had a utI ".New York Magazine, Vulture.   

On a final note if Neil Gaiman is guilty of these crimes he should face the highest extent of the law, but  there should be sufficient evidence by his victims at least attempting legal action and overall in the case to conclude that he is guilty. Anyone can claim anything, but should a mans life in general be ruined over heresay of accusers.

reply_content: Art doesnt exist separate from it´s creator or the enviorment in which it was created. Everything in an artists life; their actions, opinions and experiences all influence what they write and how they write it. That doesnt mean it cant be good (bad people can make good art like you said) but you also cant understand it fully without understanding the context in which it came about. For example; YOu cannot understand the Cthulu mythos without understanding that Lovecraft was a massive racist even for his time and his fear of that which was different from himself (black people) bleeds into his work. Likwise, knowing Gaimans actions shines a completly new light on basically everything he has written. Doesnt make it not good or not enjoyable. But to say it´s a mistake to conflate the author and his work is wrong when they are inherently tied.

reply_content:
From what I’ve read, Gaiman was raised in an extremely abusive cult environment as his parents were high-ranking members. I think this is important context as to understand _why_ a person like him exists. His work and attitude in life is deeply intertwined with the spiritual abuse perpetuated by growing up in such a screwed-up community. From my understanding, his latest book is basically a giant metaphor for his childhood and the trauma inflicted upon him by the cult environment.

Obviously, not every person who has ever survived a cult or endured severe childhood trauma has turned into a sexual predator, but I think Gaiman’s body of work contrasted with his actions serves as an important reminder as to what factors led to him becoming such a monster. People are products of their surroundings, you know? I don’t doubt that being brought up in an environment where one’s mind, body and soul were considered the property of a spiritual dictatorship contributed to a mindset that led him to desire to subjugate and control and abuse other people in turn.

It’s just sad. I don’t mean to try and justify his actions or cast him as a blameless victim, just that his own victimhood prompted him to perpetuate violence and harm upon innocent people in turn. It seems that the cult lifestyle taught him to be a manipulator and take pleasure in non consensual sexual sadism.

I think that it is worthwhile to examine both his artwork and his actions in that light in order to better understand the issue in its entirety. Burying one’s head in the sand and acting like predators simply spontaneously become predators because they are inherently evil people from the start is flawed thinking.
